question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what alternatives to weed killer his Department has researched as part of its plan to reduce the use of weed killer.
answer_text: While the Department has not carried out any specific research on alternatives to weedkiller, this topic has been discussed with colleagues from other roads authorities at the UK Roads Leadership Group, and some of its subcommittees, however, to date no effective alternative has been identified.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether Roundup, or a glyphosate-based weed killer, is used by his Department in the spraying of roadside verges.
answer_text: Chemical control of weeds by my Department is generally carried out on kerbed and paved areas by external contractors using herbicide products suitable for use on hard surfaces and listed on the Pesticides Register of UK Authorised Products, which can be viewed on the Health and Safety Executive website. I understand that Glyphosate-based products are currently used by my Department and external contractors on hard surfaces for the control of weeds to ensure road safety, to prevent structural damage to infrastructure assets, for watercourse maintenance and to fulfil legislative requirements on the control of noxious weeds and invasive species.Spraying of highway surfaces / edges is carried out by trained personnel in accordance with advice from manufacturers and the Department of Agriculture, Environment & Rural Affairs, using the minimum treatment necessary to achieve the required results.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether Roundup, or a glyphosate-based weed killer, was used in (i) May 2024; and (ii) June 2024 in the South Down constituency.
answer_text: I can confirm that glyphosate-based products were used in the South Down area in May and June 2024.Chemical control of weeds by my Department is generally carried out on kerbed and paved areas by external contractors using herbicide products suitable for use on hard surfaces and listed on the Pesticides Register of UK Authorised Products, which can be viewed on the Health and Safety Executive (GB) website.The control of weeds is to ensure road safety, to prevent structural damage to infrastructure assets, for watercourse maintenance and to fulfil legislative requirements on the control of noxious weeds and invasive species.

To ask the Minister for Communities to outline what support his Department can provide to grassroots sports clubs to become environmentally sustainable.
answer_text: Sport NI, an arm’s length body of my Department is currently working with thirty-five grassroots sports clubs which have been successful in securing National Lottery funding from Sport NI’s Renewable Energy Fund totalling over eighty thousand pounds.  This is a pilot investment programme which seeks to help sports clubs become more environmentally sustainable.In addition, Sport NI has issued an Environmental Sustainability Survey and an impact flooding survey to all Sports Clubs to establish the level of need in the sector.  Responses to these surveys will inform plans to bring further support to sports clubs over the next five years including an Environmental Training Programme for Sports Clubs and a future Investment Fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether the full budget set aside for the Enhanced Flooding Support Scheme will be allocated.
answer_text: Delivery of the Enhanced Flood Support Scheme is affordable within the funding envelope provided.  Once the final costs of the scheme is known, I will consider options for how any remaining budget will be allocated.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when compensation from the Enhanced Flood Support Scheme will be distributed to businesses affected by the flooding in October 2023 and November 2023.
answer_text: The local councils are responsible for making payments under the Enhanced Flood Support Scheme (EFSS) which is funded by the Department for the Economy. The latest figures from the local Councils, in respect of the EFSS, indicated that there was a total of 108 applications. As of 3 June, the councils have issued letters of offer to 27 businesses to the value of £1.6m and advise they have made total payments of £310k to 22 businesses. Further payments will be issued to businesses by the Councils upon receipt of a signed letter of offer and any other required documentation.The council have advised my officials that they are working with businesses on the ground to provide the appropriate evidence to receive payment.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount that has been awarded through the Enhanced Flood Support Scheme to date.
answer_text: The local councils are responsible for making payments under the Enhanced Flood Support Scheme (EFSS) which is funded by the Department for the Economy. The latest figures from the local Councils, in respect of the EFSS, indicated that there was a total of 108 eligible applications. As of 3 June, the councils have issued letters of offer to 27 businesses to the value of £1.6m and advise they have made total payments of £310k to 22 businesses. Further payments will be issued to businesses by the Councils upon receipt of a signed letter of offer and any other required documentation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether the amount required to fund the Enhanced Flood Support Scheme will come through her Department's general budget allocation.
answer_text: All of the funding requirement for Enhanced Flood Support Scheme will be fully funded via my Department’s general budget allocation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the total amount of money available for the Enhanced Flood Support Scheme.
answer_text: In February 2024, I announced that I would make up to £10 million available to support small and medium sized businesses impacted by the flooding of late October/early November 2023.  This support was in addition to the £7,500 flood support grant and rate relief that had already been provided.  The Enhanced Flood Support Scheme is one of three schemes funded in full by my Department that are currently being delivered by district councils.
